The rise and fall of Tensor

2022:
> two ex-quant traders with no funding
> win two Solana hackathons back to back
> bootstrap with $60k prize money
> launch during the FTX collapse
> zero users, zero revenue, zero social presence

2023:
> raise $3M
> quietly become #1 NFT marketplace on Solana
> beat Magic Eden in volume
> launch Tensorians NFT at 1.69 SOL

late 2023:
> Solana season begins
> pioneering cNFTs
> Tensorians hit 85 SOL (50x from mint)
> capturing 85% of all Solana NFT volume
> the "Blur of Solana" narrative is real

2024:
> TNSR token launches over $1B FDV
> everyone celebrates
> volumes go down
> farmers leave Tensor for Magic Eden

2025:
> NFT market dies
> daily volume down 80% from peak
> $20k daily fees
> team builds a new platform (Vector)
> pivot more and more to memecoins
> Vector adopts $TNSR (new hope)

2025 (Coinbase acquisition):
> Coinbase acquired Vector just to shut it down
> Coinbase takes over core team
> Tensor and $TNSR token not part of the deal
> $TNSR pumped 8-11x before announcement
> founder allocations were relocked for additional 3 years

2026:
> Tensor foundation leads marketplace now
> last tweet 4 months ago
> revenue down to $250/day
> future unclear...

covering @reflectmoney on Solana: a whitelabel stablecoin infrastructure  

how it works: it’s a new type of a stablecoin issuer. instead of issuing actual stables, Reflect issues interest-generating versions of USDC / USDT. it means that the yield comes from onchain strategies. the platform lets anyone issue their yield generating dollars with no MM & LP costs. 

USDC+ : it’s Reflect’s flagship version of USDC, producing interest rates with no lockups. it works by supplying USDC to overcollateralised lending pools, where borrowers pay interest to access liquidity. 

background: the team won Solana’s hackathon, Colosseum, in 2024 and has raised $3.75M led by a16z accelerator. 

partnerships & integrations: so far the team has onboarded Umbra to issue their yield-generating dollars, as well as secured partnerships with Moto & Titan.

anime-sol, 43.78%. this is the tight bid-ask flip wide strat and it's one of my favorite plays on a strong runner.

the setup has two phases.

phase 1: tight bid-ask. you need a strong runner with huge volume and solid narrative that's already dumped around 40% from ath. 

pool link : https://t.co/Yf52Rgoclv

that's your entry window. deploy bid-ask with a tight range, -7% to -15% depending on how confident you are in the token. the goal here is simple — let your SOL convert to tokens as price sweeps through your bins on the way down.

once your SOL is fully converted to tokens, you close the position.

phase 2: flip to token side wide. open a new position using token side only with a 100-300% range to the upside. this wide range lets you DCA in and out as the token recovers. 

you're now sitting with tokens, collecting fees as the price moves back up through your range.

why not just use a normal wide range from the start? because on a strong runner with good volume the TVL gets absolutely crowded. 

tight range on the way down gets you in efficiently before the pool thickens. flipping to wide on token side gives you the recovery play without competing with everyone else for the same bins.

the whole thing only works if the narrative is genuinely strong. if the token is just a one-day wonder this falls apart at phase 2. pick the right token and the recovery almost always comes.

Tiagogo explains his current strategy and why he sticks to wide pools

“My current strategy, I want to say maybe the one I use the most is very similar to the EvilPanda strategy. Just playing very wide. I'm always the wide guy. I always open minimum 90% and usually works for me. A big dump and then recovering a little bit of a rebounce and then you end up in profits.”

“Recently, you know, the meta is changing like every month. And now March has been drier than January, February. But I think something that's been playing also in the French community last week has been more quick in and out plays so quick in and out plays but also using spot strategy classic SOL only, not buying any token and just staying for five minutes.”

“But then you really have to find the volume play. We've been using a tool also that a guy has developed just to find the good pools. And we pray every time that these plays happen that we have a 5% or 10% pool because we've been really frustrated by the 1% or the 2% pool when this volume plays on 100K shitters that are there and you see that a guy has opened the 0.8 or 1% pool, we're like, come on, man. Why do you like to lose money?”

sampleTweets.text:
Did you know Seeker users are getting 2.5% of the @xplaceapp airdrop? 📱🪂

But how do you actually qualify?

Here’s a simple step-by-step guide to get eligible & maximize your allocation 👇

✅ Step 1: Install Correctly

→ Download @xplaceapp via the Solana dApp Store
→ This is what flags you as “eligible”
→ Web/Play Store likely won’t count for the Seeker pool

✅ Step 2: Set Up + Initial Boost

→ Sign up + connect wallet (Seed Vault)
→ Use a referral (≈500 XP bonus)
→ Complete KYC (required for card + full features)

✅ Step 3: Upgrade Once 

→ Seeker = 20% discount on memberships
→ Higher tiers = higher XP rates
→ Example: Basic: ~1% XP / Platinum: up to ~10% XP
→ This is where most of your airdrop weight comes from

✅ Step 4: Actually Use It

→ This is not a passive airdrop, you need activity
→ Deposit collateral (earn yield + XP)
→ Spend via Credit Mode (XP + USDC cashback)
→ Use it daily - consistency compounds XP fastest

✅ Step 5: Referrals = Hidden Edge

→ Earn ~40% of referral membership in USDC
→ Get bonus XP tied to their activity
→ This can outperform your own usage if done right

✅ Step 6: Stay Active Until Snapshot

→ XP = your airdrop score
→ The 2.5% pool is shared pro-rata among Seekers
→ More XP = bigger slice

✅ What most people miss

→ It’s a separate allocation pool (not just a boost)
→ Only Seeker users via the dApp Store qualify
→ Early users have a massive advantage
→ You’re still earning from the main pool
→ But Seeker users tap into an extra 2.5% pool on top
→ Same XP → eligible across 2 pools

Crazy to see how almost every airdropped token has gone straight down this cycle.

Airdrops are great for user acquisition, marketing & rewarding early users.

But clearly, something in the design is broken.

Most launches start too high, with no real demand from day one.

Maybe the answer is simpler:

👉 Launch at (much) lower valuations
👉 Give tokens room to grow
👉 Align holders with long-term upside
👉 Have real demand drivers ready at TGE

oke sekarang gimana cara build trading agent atau agent lain dari scratch.

hal pertama yang gua lakuin setelah subscribe Claude adalah ngambil course ini. gratis. link di komen

Anthropic Academy punya 16 course sekarang. dari yang paling basic kayak Claude 101 dan AI Fluency, sampe yang teknikal banget kayak Building with the Claude API, MCP, agent skills, dan subagents.

gua mulai dari AI Fluency dulu buat ngerti cara kerja LLM dari dasar. terus Claude 101 buat tau fitur apa aja yang tersedia. dan itu aja udah cukup buat gua mulai build hal-hal yang gua mau.

kalo lu mau tau cara maksimalin Claude atau mau mulai build sesuatu di atas Claude, ini starting point yang paling bener menurut gua.

beberapa hari ini gua switch otak agent gua ke Minimax.

alasannya simpel: lebih murah dan cukup banget buat jalanin Meridian dan agent-trenching gua sehari-hari.

dengan $10 sebulan, agent gua sekarang bisa hasilin sekitar $50 per hari. 

bukan karena model-nya mahal, tapi karena agent-nya udah "belajar" dari kesalahan sebelumnya dan adapt sama kondisi market.  otak yang lebih murah bisa perform bagus kalau data dan lesson-nya bener.

sebelumnya gua pakai OpenRouter dengan pay-per-use. buat satu agent itu masih oke dan lebih fleksibel. tapi karena gua udah multi-agent dan request-nya mulai banyak, biaya pay-per-use mulai ga efisien.

gua switch ke Minimax token plan yang baru diluncurin. max 1500 request per 5 jam. dari data usage gua di OpenRouter, per 24 jam gua ngabisin sekitar 2000 request buat 2 agents.

oh dan gua juga pakai Minimax buat ngoding tipis-tipis kalau Claude lagi di peak hour buat ngirit limit.

$10 sebulan buat agent yang hasilin 50$ per hari. math-nya gut.

ini agent dlmm gua yang ngehasilin duit 20-100$ sehari. gua open source, gua kasih tau cara setupnya, simak aja videonya.

meridian ini udah jalan 2 minggu, banyak juga temen temen yang udah profit dan atau masih struggle loss buat train agent mereka.

ada repo githubnya juga. kalian bisa cek github dan tele channel untuk detail fiturnya.

ini bukan ajakan finansial atau nfa. dan selalu kasi ilmu dulu ke diri kalian bagaimana agent ini bekerja.

edge dari agent ini adalah gimana cara kalian ngasih lessons ke meridian.

cheer to @mininghelium1 for help me improving this agent.

bikin agent yang bisa hasilin lu duit itu gampang banget. tapi cara yang paling enak buat orang non teknikal itu gini.

gua pake claude ya, kalo gaada bisa pake gemini yang cli, lagi ada diskon 3rb doang perbulan.

lu siapin flownya, datanya ambil dari mana aja.

bilang juga interview gua sampe lu ngerti 95% apa yang pengen gua buat sebenernya, jadi claude lu ga nebak nebak doang.

contoh di sini gua mau bikin listener buat agent gua, jadi dia bisa tau orang orang di dc lparmy/meteoraidn lagi pada cari pool apa.

gua suruh crosscheck dulu, kalo ada kesamaan ca di 2 server dia bakal ngirim sinyal ke agent gua buat di analyze.

ngodingnya suruh aja claude, lu tinggal approve kalo udah sesuai.

riset data presisinya dan flownya, lu bakal bisa build apa yang lu pengen.

setelah gua build beberapa agent, gua baru sadar kalo sesuatu yang paling penting adalah

dataset.

lu bisa punya LLM terbaik, agent loop yang paling clean, tooling yang paling canggih. tanpa dataset yang bener, agent lu ga bisa ngapa-ngapain yang berguna.

LLM belum bisa baca web secara native kayak manusia. tapi mereka baca markdown files dan JSON dengan sangat baik. di situlah dataset lu harus hidup. terstruktur, bersih, dan bisa diproses sama model.

kalau lu mau agent lu ngerti tentang suatu platform atau protokol, cek dulu docs-nya. terus cek apakah mereka nyediain API reference. kalau ada, itu sumber data lu. feed ke agent lu dalam format yang bisa dia proses.

makanya kenapa sekarang banyak startup yang berlomba-lomba buat "bayar" lu buat ngasih data ke mereka. 

build data pipeline-nya dulu. agent itu cuma layer di atasnya.

trashcan-sol, twice. $145 in 1 minute 8 seconds. $83 in 2 minutes 12 seconds. not my usual play but this volume was too good to ignore.

the setup: single side sol, 1 bin only. that's it.

but before anything else you need the right pool. this only works on 100/5 or 125/10 pools. anything with a 2% base fee won't give you enough to make this worthwhile.

the mechanic is simple. when your 1 bin fills and then fills back out, you're capturing roughly 2x the base fee in that single movement. on a 5% base fee pool that's around 10%, on a 10% pool you're looking at 20%+ in a single fill cycle. that's what the cards show.

volume needs to be in the 100k-200k per 5 minutes range for the bin to cycle fast enough. below that and you're just sitting there waiting.

the catch: you need to be completely locked in. eyes on the chart the whole time. when the bin fills you need to know immediately, and when it fills back out you're done, close and move on. this is not a set-and-forget play. one distraction and you miss the exit.

also prepare your mental before trying this. watching a single bin move in real time while $100+ is sitting in it is a different experience from holding a normal range position.

$228 in under 4 minutes total. gut strat for the right market conditions.

We were losing users until we made this change.

Previously, we used our own built-in verification system on @NOMADZxyz.

However, many users were hesitant to connect their wallets, which created friction during the verification process.

We stayed in this situation for a while, but when we were preparing to onboard the @MonkeDAO community, they also expressed concerns about our verification method and the safety of their community members.

To solve this issue they recommended @MatricaLabs, as they were already partners with them.

As Solana SMB Gen 2 and Gen3 holders, we were able to secure a 75% discount on the Matrica Enterprise package.

Since then, our users can verify their NFTs off-chain using Discord, Telegram, and other platforms, without needing to connect their wallets directly.

We already have following communities live on Nomadz:

> @RadiantsDAO
> @SolanaSensei
> @Perenimals
> @SolanaMBS
> @sagamonkes
> @islanddao
> @superteam
> @MadLads

Go to Nomadz → Profile → Communities → Verify with Matrica.

You’ll receive 200 XP + a 20% XP boost once you complete verification.

reason: Infra / MEV cluster linked by [validator, solana, mev, long]

sampleTweets.text:
Anza dropped constellations, a multi-proposer protocol for Solana with 50ms cycles.

Multiple validators compete to include your transaction every 50 milliseconds, so no single one can block or reorder it for profit

No more single-leader monopoly. Censorship resistance baked into the protocol itself.

MEV mitigation without trusting any third party.

This is what decentralized NASDAQ actually looks like at the infra level

TLDR: Bullish af for Solana.

The FCFS design is simple.  Elect one big box that offers the best fee to the validators for long epoch, like 2 days, and let validators rotate the box on failure at any time.  We can have one giant proposer in Tokyo or wherever it is the most profitable to do so.

The current setup, where it is neither optimal for fcfs or mcp is the worst of both worlds.

🚨ALERT: According to @SocketSecurity, a supply chain attack on Axios, a widely used JavaScript library that lets apps and websites communicate with servers, resulted in hidden malware being injected into its npm distribution by an unknown attacker. 

The attack could impact developers, companies, and applications relying on Axios, including many crypto projects, potentially exposing systems and sensitive data. The malware can affect macOS, Windows, and Linux systems, making the risk widespread given Axios is used across millions of projects globally.
